<p align="center">
    <img src="https://github.com/rose-pine/rose-pine-theme/raw/main/assets/icon.png" width="80" alt="" />
    <h2 align="center">Rosé Pine for Glamour</h2>
</p>

<p align="center">All natural pine, faux fur and a bit of soho vibes for the classy minimalist</p>

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD045 -->
<p align="center">
    <a href="https://github.com/rose-pine/rose-pine-theme">
        <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/community-rosé%20pine-26233a?labelColor=191724&logo=data:image/svg+xml;base64,&style=for-the-badge" />
    </a>
</p>

Glamour styles for terminal markdown — the rendering engine behind [**Glow**](https://github.com/charmbracelet/glow),
GitHub CLI, and other Charm tools. Colors follow the official [**Rosé Pine**](https://rosepinetheme.com/) palette, with
syntax roles aligned to [rose-pine-bat](https://github.com/drluckyspin/rose-pine-bat).

## TL;DR

```bash
make check
make build
make test
glow -s styles/rose-pine-moon-dark.json examples/sample.md
```

## Variants

| Style name            | File                                                                 | Description              |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `rose-pine`           | [`styles/rose-pine.json`](styles/rose-pine.json)                     | Rosé Pine main (dark)    |
| `rose-pine-moon`      | [`styles/rose-pine-moon.json`](styles/rose-pine-moon.json)           | Rosé Pine Moon           |
| `rose-pine-moon-dark` | [`styles/rose-pine-moon-dark.json`](styles/rose-pine-moon-dark.json) | Moon syntax on main base |
| `rose-pine-dawn`      | [`styles/rose-pine-dawn.json`](styles/rose-pine-dawn.json)           | Rosé Pine Dawn (light)   |

## Usage

### Glow

```bash
glow -s /absolute/path/to/styles/rose-pine-moon-dark.json README.md
```

Or set `style:` in `glow.yml` to an absolute path (tilde expansion is unreliable in TUI mode).

### Glamour (Go)

```go
import "github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"

r, _ := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
    glamour.WithStylesFromJSONFile("styles/rose-pine.json"),
)
out, _ := r.Render("# Hello\n")
```

### Environment

```bash
export GLAMOUR_STYLE="/absolute/path/to/styles/rose-pine.json"
glow README.md
```

## Developing

Go sources live under [`styles/`](styles/) and mirror the layout expected by
[charmbracelet/glamour](https://github.com/charmbracelet/glamour) for upstream contribution.

Requires `jq` and `go` (`brew install jq go`). Optional: `glow` for `make preview`; `python3` + Pillow for
`make screenshots` (`brew install pillow`).

```bash
make help       # list targets (Rosé Pine–themed output)
make check      # verify tools
make build      # regenerate styles/*.json from Go sources
make test       # validate JSON + Glamour render (scripts/verify)
make screenshots   # regenerate gallery PNGs
make preview STYLE=rose-pine-moon-dark   # preview examples/sample.md
make clean      # remove Python caches and Go test cache
```

Edit palette roles in [`styles/palette.go`](styles/palette.go) and shared element mapping in
[`styles/builder.go`](styles/builder.go), then run `make build`.

## Upstreaming

Built-in Glow styles are defined in Glamour, not Glow. To contribute:

1. Open an issue on [charmbracelet/glamour](https://github.com/charmbracelet/glamour) describing which variants to
   include.
2. Copy the relevant `styles/*.go` files and register them in Glamour's `styles/styles.go`.
3. Run Glamour's `go generate ./...` and add gallery screenshots.

## Thanks to

- [charmbracelet/glamour](https://github.com/charmbracelet/glamour) and [glow](https://github.com/charmbracelet/glow)
- [Rosé Pine](https://github.com/rose-pine) for the palette

## See also

If you're looking for a Glow-focused installer and `glow.yml` workflow, see
[rose-pine-glow](https://github.com/drluckyspin/rose-pine-glow). This repo is the canonical Glamour style source (Go →
JSON).

## Contributing

Issues and pull requests are welcome. For palette changes, stay aligned with the
[official Rosé Pine palette](https://github.com/rose-pine/palette).
